Discover what You may not have known...
Akira Toriyama

Searching through DBZ web pages on the Internet, you may be lucky to find 1 or 2 pages that even have a small section of information on the creator of Dragon Ball, Akira Toriyama. Ive gathered information on him from few magazines, sites dedicated to him, and even sites dedicated to video games. He is well known for his artistic talents and I thought Id share some of his accomplishments with you.

Akira Toriyama is a well known artist in the manga/anime community of Japan. He is best known for the creation of the Dragon Ball series which is famous in parts of Asia as well as North America. Although Dragon Ball is one of his most widely recognized work, some critics would argue that it is not his best. He lives with his family in Tokyo, Japan.

Toriyama went to highschool to study art specializing in publicity. In 1977 he decided to abandon this to enroll in cartoon drawing. In 1978 he entered Shueisha, the biggest manga publisher in Japan, as a cartoonist, where he published his first work as a professional, "Wonder Island." Dragon Ball was first published in 1983 by Shueisha Inc.

Dragon Ball first began television syndication in Japan in 1988 (the same year as Ranma 1/2). Dragon Ball Z first began syndication in America in 1995 (as well as Sailor Moon, Ronin Warriors, Samurai Pizza Cats and Teknoman).

Toriyama has contributed in artistic design of videogames such as Dragon Quest, Squaresoft's Tobal No. 1, Chrono Trigger and Chrono Cross. Toriyama's success in the fields of anime/manga and video games has made him popular with a diverse demographic. Fans of Toriyama hope for more of his talent to be shared with the public.



Points of Interest:
Akira Toriyama's World
